task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal application is open with 'foothills_3d_site.tcp' visible in the title bar, recent files, or project explorer.

task_completion.description: Select the 3D polylines and breaklines in the drawing
task_completion.visual_evidence: Surveyed lines (polylines/breaklines) are highlighted or selected within the TopoCal graphical viewport.

task_completion.description: Flatten the selected lines by setting their Z-elevations to 0.00
task_completion.visual_evidence: Interaction with a tool, menu (e.g., 'Pasar a 2D'), or properties panel in the Spanish TopoCal UI that sets the Z-coordinate/elevation of the lines to 0.00.

task_completion.description: Initiate the export of the 2D drawing to an AutoCAD DXF format
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or 'Save As' dialog is open in TopoCal with the file type explicitly set to AutoCAD DXF.

task_completion.description: Save the exported DXF file to the exact specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the directory 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\' and the file name 'architectural_basemap_2d.dxf' being entered or confirmed.

integrity.description: Agent used the TopoCal graphical user interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through TopoCal menus, toolbars, and dialogs rather than using a terminal, PowerShell, or Python script to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent modified the existing surveyed lines rather than deleting and redrawing them
integrity.visual_evidence: The original geometry and complexity of the lines remain intact during the flattening process; there is no evidence of the agent deleting the lines and manually drawing new ones.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely exported the file from TopoCal rather than creating a dummy file
integrity.visual_evidence: The TopoCal export/save dialog is actively used to create the DXF file, rather than the agent opening a text editor or terminal to create an empty file named 'architectural_basemap_2d.dxf'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path for the DXF file is exactly 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\architectural_basemap_2d.dxf'. The resulting file must contain a minimum of 10 entities. Note that the TopoCal 2025 interface is in Spanish, so the agent should be interacting with Spanish menu items (e.g., look for 'Pasar a 2D' or similar Spanish terminology for flattening).
